summary: Canonical SAT dedup is a method packet normalizing 106 structured analytic techniques across 11 catalogues.
final_use_case: It matters because a SAT studio needs a deduplicated method canon before it can author, test, combine, or adapt techniques for human, AI, and human-AI workflows.

instruments.instrument_name: Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Handoff Identity
instruments.input: source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.operation: Canonical SAT dedup is a method packet normalizing 106 structured analytic techniques across 11 catalogues.
instruments.output: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept source packet, code-ready room description, scope, and intended runtime and produce bounded definition of what the handoff asks a builder to construct.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Handoff Identity; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.instrument_name: Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Room Task Model
instruments.input: user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects
instruments.operation: It reconciles the Georgetown Registry, IC compendium, SAT and alternative-analysis traditions, red-team methods, CIA Tradecraft Primer, Army ATP, NATO alternative-analysis material, UK MOD red teaming, UFMCS Red Team Handbook, Morgan Jones, and normalized N-001 through N-106 records.
instruments.output: room logic that turns the concept into a repeatable workflow
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires user roles, task sequence, room state, and domain objects
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds room logic that turns the concept into a repeatable workflow; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.

instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Room Task Model; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.

instruments.instrument_name: Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Data Schema And Persistence Plan
instruments.input: entities, records, IDs, tables, JSON structures, indexes, and state transitions
instruments.operation: Intelligence tradecraft, counterintelligence, and analytic method. Use source library intelligence titles as method authority and intelligence-domain stressors. Vision: Present intelligence pages as disciplined analytic-method and tradecraft demonstrations. Mechanism: Tie claims to collection, covert action, deception, interrogation, bias-control, and analytic-logic mechanisms. Plan: Prioritize Capstone, SATS, CI, RAG integrity, and analyst-assurance pages for close-reading. Status: source library identified as.
instruments.output: data model that lets the room preserve work instead of acting like a static page

instruments.instrument_name: Canonical SAT dedup: 106 techniques normalized across 11 catalogues Integration Path
instruments.input: frontend, backend, storage, retrieval, auth, export, and portfolio linkage
instruments.operation: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. 1. Given a technique name seen in any primary SAT reference — grep Canonical_Name or Aliases/Variants — resolve to Norm_ID. 2. Given a Norm_ID — read Sources to find every catalogue that lists this technique. 3. Filter on Category to get curated technique families ("Challenge & Perspective Shift" for red-cell challenge sessions; "Decomposition & Representation" for problem-structuring stages). 4. Join against.
instruments.output: build sequence for turning the handoff into working software
